steps.title: Express the target as a linear combination
steps.content:
We want to find $\frac{17}{2}x + 18y$ and notice it can be written as $a\left(\frac{7}{2}x + 6y\right) + b\left(\frac{5}{2}x + 6y\right)$ for some constants $a$ and $b$.

Expanding: $\frac{7a + 5b}{2}x + 6(a+b)y$

Matching coefficients:
  • Coefficient of $x$: $\frac{7a + 5b}{2} = \frac{17}{2}$ gives $7a + 5b = 17$
  • Coefficient of $y$: $6(a + b) = 18$ gives $a + b = 3$

From $a + b = 3$, we get $b = 3 - a$. Substitute into the first equation:
$$7a + 5(3 - a) = 17$$
$$7a + 15 - 5a = 17$$
$$2a = 2$$
$$a = 1 \text{ and } b = 2$$

steps.title: Apply the linear combination
steps.content:
Since $(x, y)$ satisfies both original equations, we can use the same linear combination on their values:
$$\frac{17}{2}x + 18y = 1(25) + 2(23) = 25 + 46 = 71$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
